WEEK NUMBER GUIDE

ISO Week Number Explained

An ISO week number is a standardized way to number the weeks of the year, defined by the ISO 8601 international standard. Week 1 is the week containing the first Thursday of the year — and every week runs Monday to Sunday.

Calendars
Week Numbers 2025·Week Numbers 2026·Week Numbers 2027

What is an ISO week number?

ISO week numbering assigns a number from 1 to 52 (or 53) to each week of the year. It is defined by the ISO 8601 standard, used in business, logistics, and government across Europe and internationally.

Key rules:

  • Weeks run Monday to Sunday
  • Week 1 is the week containing the first Thursday of January
  • Most years have 52 weeks; some have 53
  • The week year and the calendar year can differ by 1–3 days at year boundaries

How is week 1 defined?

Week 1 is the week that contains January 4. This is equivalent to saying: the week containing the first Thursday of the year.

This means January 1 is sometimes in week 52 or 53 of the previous year. For example:

  • January 1, 2016 was a Friday → it belonged to week 53 of 2015
  • January 1, 2018 was a Monday → week 1 of 2018 started that day

Conversely, late December days can fall in week 1 of the next year. December 31, 2018 was a Monday — it was in week 1 of 2019.

Why do some years have 53 weeks?

A regular year has 52 weeks and 1 extra day. A leap year has 52 weeks and 2 extra days. When January 1 falls on a Thursday, the year has 53 ISO weeks. Leap years that start on a Wednesday also get 53 weeks.

Recent years with 53 ISO weeks: 2015, 2020, 2026, 2032.

ISO week numbering vs US week numbering

ISO 8601 (international)US / Canada
First day of weekMondaySunday
Week 1 definitionContains January 4 (first Thursday)Contains January 1
Used inEurope, business, logisticsUSA, Canada, Japan
Years with 53 weeksYears starting on Thu (or Wed in leap)Years starting on Sun (or Sat in leap)

FAQ

What week number is it today?

Use the week number calculator on this site — it shows the current ISO week number updated in real time.

Does week 1 always start on January 1?

No. ISO week 1 starts on Monday of the week containing January 4. January 1 can fall in week 52 or 53 of the previous year if it is a Friday, Saturday, or Sunday.

How many weeks are in 2025?

2025 has 52 ISO weeks. Week 1 of 2025 started on Monday, December 30, 2024. Week 52 ends on Sunday, December 28, 2025.

How many weeks are in 2026?

2026 has 53 ISO weeks, because January 1, 2026 falls on a Thursday. Week 53 of 2026 contains December 28–January 3, 2027.

What is the difference between week number and calendar week?

"Week number" and "calendar week" (KW in German, CW in English) both refer to ISO week numbers — they are the same thing. The term "calendar week" is common in German-speaking countries and business contexts.

Related: How many weeks in a year? · Week numbers 2025 calendar · What week number is it?
